commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ed9acb051965fe379ab161c567a684ddcf476b79)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates the DeepInfra plugin, docs, changelog, and tests to add live per-surface model discovery, image/video catalog providers, a DeepInfra Anthropic cache wrapper, and revised DeepInfra defaults.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection is enough for the main review risks: current main's DeepInfra defaults and cache wrapper path are visible, and the PR diff visibly changes fallback/default behavior. I did not run the PR because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human handling is needed because the remaining blockers combine contributor proof requirements with maintainer approval for default/fallback compatibility.

target.title: fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse t…
target.body: # fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse them during onboarding ## Summary - **Problem**: DeepInfra plugin pinned the chat catalog to 7 hardcoded manifest entries (a wizard-vs-discovery guard short-circuited discovery once the auth wizard seeded models), had no live discovery seam for image-gen / video-gen, and silently dropped Anthropic `cache_control` markers when proxying `anthropic/*` models — prompt caching was advertised eligible but never fired. - **Solution**: Wire DeepInfra's `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` (the agent-tagged projection now shipping in the backend) through a per-surface helper that buckets responses by short-alias tag (`chat`/`vlm`/`embed`/`image-gen`/`video-gen`/`tts`/`stt`). Register live image-gen and video-gen catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern). Replace the OpenRouter-only Anthropic cache wrapper with a deepinfra-local one gated on the model id. - **What changed**: new `surface-model-catalogs.ts` (live image-gen + video-gen catalog providers + `resolveModelCapabilities`); new `cache-wrapper.ts` (Anthropic ephemeral cache marker injection for `anthropic/*` on DeepInfra); refactored `provider-models.ts` (per-surface bucketed discovery, API-key gated, 5-min cache, sync static fallback); media-provider builders now accept the per-surface catalog; `index.ts` feeds all media surfaces from one source + registers the new catalog providers; `media-models.ts` `DEFAULT_*` constants demoted to `*_FALLBACK_MODELS`; TTS default bumped to `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`. - **Scope boundary**: No edits outside `extensions/deepinfra/`. No SDK changes (`src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts` is byte-identical to `main`). TTS/STT/VLM/embed surfaces still use static fallback only — extending `UnifiedModelCatalogKind` to cover them is a separate SDK PR. ## Motivation DeepInfra ships hundreds of models, but openclaw only surfaced 7 hardcoded chat IDs — every new model needed a code change here. The DeepInfra backend now emits surface-tagged responses on `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta`, so the plugin can map tags → surfaces dynamically. Anthropic prompt caching on DeepInfra was also silently broken: the runner advertised eligibility but the OpenRouter-only stream wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so `cache_control` markers never went out. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Linked Issue/PR - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof ### Environment Hosted Lightsail VM `ip-172-26-8-116` (3.71.115.124), Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.15.0, openclaw 2026.5.19, branch `ed9acb0519`. Live network to `api.deepinfra.com`. DeepInfra key configured via auth-profile store + `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` env (both paths exercised). `openclaw.service` live state: ``` Active: active (running) Memory: 582.9M (max 3.5G) Python helper sidecar: PID 1388 (fs-safe pinned writes enabled) ``` - **Steps after this patch**: ```bash pnpm build pnpm test extensions/deepinfra rm -rf ~/.openclaw-dev DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability model providers | grep deepinfra DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability image providers | grep deepinfra DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability audio providers | grep deepinfra ``` - **Evidence after fix**: - `capability model providers` → `{"provider":"deepinfra","count":88,"defaults":["anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5","anthropic/claude-opus-4-7","anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"],"available":true,...}` (was: 7 on `main`). - `capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l` → 88 (was: 7). - `capability image providers` → deepinfra row with `FLUX-1-schnell` default + fallback allowlist. - `capability audio providers` → `{"id":"deepinfra","configured":true,"capabilities":["image","audio"],"defaultModels":{"image":"moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5","audio":"openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo"}}`. - `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` → 59/59. - `pnpm check:changed` → exit 0 (one pre-existing unrelated lint failure in `scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs:398`, last touched in `85f9276624`). - **Not tested**: end-to-end TTS/STT audio round-trip and a real image/video-gen request against the live backend. The runtime providers register correctly and discovery returns the right shape; downstream inference was not exercised. ## Root Cause - (a) `augmentModelCatalog` short-circuited with `existingDeepInfraCount > DEEPINFRA_MODEL_CATALOG.length`, skipping discovery whenever the auth wizard had seeded entries first. - (b) Media surfaces had no `registerModelCatalogProvider` wiring. - (c) `wrapStreamFn` reused `createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper`, which short-circuits on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so Anthropic ephemeral cache markers never reached the payload for DeepInfra-routed Anthropic models even though `isCacheTtlEligible` returned `true`. **Missing guardrail**: no test asserted that `wrapStreamFn` applied `cache_control` markers for `anthropic/*` ids; no test asserted that the augmented catalog grew past the manifest size on successful discovery. ## Regression Test Plan - **Coverage level**: [x] Unit test [x] Seam / integration test - **Target tests**: - `extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts` (new) — asserts `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers are applied for `anthropic/*` ids and skipped otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/surface-model-catalogs.test.ts` (new) — asserts the live catalog providers return `null` without a key, return `null` on empty surface buckets, and project entries with the correct shape otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts` (extended) — per-surface bucketing + manifest fallback path. - `extensions/deepinfra/index.test.ts` (extended) — augmentModelCatalog merges discovered + wizard-seeded entries instead of suppressing one. - extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts (4 tests) exercises createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper against anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 & anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 and asserts cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" } is injected into the system message under both string-content and array-content shapes; negative test confirms non-anthropic ids pass through untouched. - extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts (3 new cases) mocks openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.isProviderApiKeyConfigured and asserts hasDeepInfraApiKey({ env: {}, agentDir }) falls back to the auth-profile store with the correct provider/agentDir args, short-circuits on env var, and returns false when both env and store are empty. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - **Chat catalogue**: with `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` set, OpenClaw surfaces the full DeepInfra catalogue (88 chat + 38 vlm today across `anthropic/`, `ByteDance/`, `deepseek-ai/`, `google/`, `meta-llama/`, `Qwen/`, `zai-org/`, …). Without a key, the curated 7-model manifest fallback is served. - **Image-gen + video-gen**: a live `registerModelCatalogProvider` now refreshes per-kind catalogs from DeepInfra when a key is configured. Without a key, the curated static fallback is served. - **TTS default**: `hexgrad/Kokoro-82M` → `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`. Users on the implicit default will now hit Qwen3-TTS; Kokoro stays in the fallback list and can be selected explicitly. - **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries. - **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Multi-turn conversations should show non-zero `cache_creation_input_tokens` on turn 1 and `cache_read_input_tokens` on turn 2+. - Backward compatible; no config/env/migration changes. ## Diagram ```text Before: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds 7 manifest entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: count > MODEL_CATALOG.length? -> skip discovery] -> [picker shows 7 models] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper -> provider !== "openrouter" -> short-circuit] -> [payload sent WITHOUT cache_control markers, prompt cache never fires] After: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: always run discovery] -> [discoverDeepInfraSurfaces: GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta] -> [bucket by surface alias tag] -> [picker shows 88 chat + 38 vlm + ...] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper: model.id starts with "anthropic/" -> apply patch] -> [payload sent WITH cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}, prompt cache fires] ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — one read-only GET to `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` per process per 5 minutes, gated on `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` being configured. The endpoint is unauthenticated; the gate is a UX choice (pre-auth flows show the static fallback, no useless network traffic). No PII or secrets in the request. - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Human Verification (Observed Result) ran `pnpm dev --dev onboard` with provider deepinfra and successfully loaded all discovery models: Tested various `pnpm dev --dev capability ` commands (see section "Real behavior proof") Session logs from image gen, VLM, TTS and STT generation in the UI dashboard: {"type":"session","version":3,"id":"f26d402a-ee0a-4014-b916-6aa0579450b1","timestamp":"2026-05-23T05:30:15.655Z","cwd":"/home/openclaw"} {"type":"model_change","id":"90f78285","parentId":null,"timestamp":"2026-05-23T05:31:05.365Z","provider":"deepinfra","modelId":"deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash"} {"type":"thinking_level_change","id":"0843df49","parentId":"90f78285","timestamp":"2026-05-23T05:31:05.366Z","thinkingLevel":"medium"} {"type":"custom","customType":"model-snapshot","data":{"timestamp":1779514265390,"provider":"deepinfra","modelApi":"openai-completions","modelId":"deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash"},"id":"64a8c870","parentId":"0843df49","timestamp":"2026-05-23T05:31:05.390Z"} {"type":"message","id":"a76678f2","parentId":"64a8c870","timestamp":"2026-05-23T05:31:05.577Z","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"[Sat 2026-05-23 05:30 UTC] generate an image of a goat in a space suit"}],"timestamp":1779514265414}} {"type":"message","id":"8125706e","parentId":"a76678f2","timestamp":"2026-05-23T05:31:12.316Z","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The user wants me to generate an image of a goat in a space suit. Let me use the image_generate tool.","thinkingSignature":"reasoning_content"},{"type":"toolCall","id":"call_00_k69xSWSRl82cGA3mvVQFHhiU","name":"image_generate","arguments":{"prompt":"A goat wearing a high-tech space suit, floating in outer space with stars and a distant planet in the background, realistic style, cinematic lighting","size":"1536x1024"},"partialArgs":"{\"prompt\": \"A goat wearing a high-tech space suit, floating in outer space with stars and a distant planet in the background, realistic style, cinematic lighting\", \"size\": \"1536x1024\"}"}],"api":"openai-completions","provider":"deepinfra","model":"deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash","usage":{"input":15566,"output":118,"cacheRead":0,"cacheWrite":0,"totalTokens":15684,"cost":{"input":0.0015566000000000002,"output":0.0000236,"cacheRead":0,"cacheWrite":0,"total":0.0015802000000000001}},"stopReason":"toolUse","timestamp":1779514265895,"responseId":"chatcmpl-RSMXIWcDaN62DUDKPyJo2QDf"}} {"type":"message","id":"c2a1c674","parentId":"8125706e","timestamp":"2026-05-23T05:31:14.641Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_00_k69xSWSRl82cGA3mvVQFHhiU","toolName":"image_generate","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Background task started for image generation (73e307a3-39ce-49a6-826a-467195a63f4f). {"type":"message","id":"ed33c9a2","parentId":"893805db","timestamp":"2026-05-23T05:32:52.284Z","message":{"role":"toolResult","toolCallId":"call_00_U2qFuQ6aWMkxrDHTk4yRDixD","toolName":"image","content":[{"type":"text","text":"This surreal digital artwork depicts a goat wearing a detailed astronaut suit floating in outer space. The goat, with its curved horns and white-gray fur, peers out from inside the space helmet with a calm, almost smiling expression. The space suit is rendered with realistic textures, showing the thick fabric, metallic rings around the helmet base, and a large backpack life-support system.\n\nThe background features a cosmic scene with countless stars scattered across the dark void.
